mnemonics.title: Kernel of a Linear Transformation
mnemonics.content: The set of all vectors v such that T(v) = 0

mnemonics.title: Range of a Linear Transformation
mnemonics.content: The set of all vectors w such that w = T(v) for some v

mnemonics.title: Rank-Nullity Theorem
mnemonics.content: The rank of T plus the nullity of T is equal to the dimension of the domain

acronyms.usage: Used to describe a linear transformation
Used to describe the kernel of a linear transformation
Used to describe the range of a linear transformation

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a linear transformation as a way of stretching and shrinking vectors
Imagine the kernel of a linear transformation as the set of all vectors that get mapped to the zero vector
Imagine the range of a linear transformation as the set of all vectors that can be reached by applying the linear transformation

analogies.analogy: A linear transformation is like a recipe that takes in ingredients (vectors) and produces a new dish (transformed vectors)
The kernel of a linear transformation is like the set of all ingredients that get thrown away during the cooking process
The range of a linear transformation is like the set of all possible dishes that can be made using the recipe
